summary: Pin projects_issues to its canonical /projects/{project_id}/issues/ list, dedupe the work_items alias, and drop the deep 2nd-level dependents in sync.go.
reason: The profiler's non-deterministic list-endpoint selection (Go map iteration) emits projects_issues and projects_work_items 3x each — duplicate `case` labels are a hard compile break, and the extra sub-collection fan-outs over-sync thousands of rate-limited requests per issue. Pinning the canonical list and pruning the work_items alias + deep dependents restores the dogfooded baseline (529 records / 11 resources).
validated_outcome: Clean generate fails to compile (duplicate case projects_issues/projects_work_items); after the pin the tree builds and an isolated live sync matches baseline deterministic counts (projects 4, members 5, cycles 8, modules 17, labels 37, states 20, archived_modules 5, projects_issues ~432).

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Deterministic list-endpoint selection within a resource group, plus an x- knob to pin the canonical list and exclude sub-collections.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Profiler picks list endpoints via unordered Go map iteration, so a multi-sub-collection resource emits duplicate/incorrect dependentResourceDefs run-to-run; not spec-fixable.
